full_description: Count the randomly selected target object type, then press the number button matching the count; from left to right the buttons represent 1, 2, and 3.
schema: {A} denotes the selected target object type, {B} denotes the matching number button, {a} denotes the arm used to press the button, {x} denotes the target count
preference: Mention counting the named object type, the left-to-right 1/2/3 button mapping, and pressing the matching button once. Do not reveal the counted answer in subtask instructions.
seen: Count {A}, then press the matching button with {a}; the buttons are 1, 2, 3 from left to right.
Find how many {A} are present and answer using the left-to-right 1/2/3 buttons.
Scan the objects, count the target type, and press the matching number button.
Use {a} to press the matching button after counting {A}.
unseen: Count the selected object type and press the matching number button; left to right means 1, 2, and 3.
After counting {A}, press the corresponding button once.
Press the button whose left-to-right order equals the count of {A}.

seen: press the button matching the counted result with {a}
